data.body: Evaluated epic 3006-e198 (level-triggered bridge convergence) before implementation. Findings: (1) implementation is greenfield — no classify(), no per-binding baseline; ADRs 0026-0029 committed on feat/bridge-convergence (rebased onto origin/main cleanly). (2) No genesis session_log exists in the store; audit evidence lives in the epic + 4 child bugs (high quality: runtime proofs + commit archaeology). (3) Web research (authorized) validates the design against OSS peers: level-triggered = k8s doctrine; per-binding baseline = kubectl last-applied / Terraform state; circuit breaker = Entra Connect deletion threshold + GCDS delete limits + Argo allowEmpty; Exalate per-pair replica = same baseline pattern; convergence-as-tested-property is ahead of common controller practice. (4) Risk verdict: MEDIUM overall — no hard deletes anywhere, bindings.json committed per pass, workflow mode input = one-click brake; ONE high-risk seam = prev_snapshot -> per-binding baseline swap (silent teammate-edit clobber). (5) Derisk ladder E1-E5 (offline replay go/no-go, property suite, baseline dual-write shadow, CI shadow consumer, capped rollout) + 5 peer-pattern gaps (observedGeneration, backoff-before-quarantine, finalizers, resync knob, optimistic write precondition) recorded as a comment on the epic.
